task_description:
# Gear Quantity Tracking

Enable the existing database quantity column to track multiple units of the same gear item, allowing proper inventory counts.

## Rationale
Database column exists but is unused (known gap). Competitor PackLight users complain about inability to track quantities (pain-4-1). Essential for consumables and spares.

## User Stories
- As a backpacker, I want to track that I own 4 fuel canisters so that I know how many are available for my trip

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gear form includes quantity field with default of 1
- [ ] Inventory shows total count when quantity > 1
- [ ] Loadout builder respects available quantity
- [ ] Weight calculations multiply by quantity where applicable
- [ ] Gallery view displays quantity badge on multi-unit items

patterns.form_field_pattern: FormField with FormControl wrapping shadcn/ui Input component, using react-hook-form control prop
patterns.validation_pattern: Zod schema with z.number().int().min(1).default(1) for quantity field
patterns.state_management_pattern: Zustand store with optimistic updates and Supabase sync
patterns.badge_pattern: shadcn/ui Badge component, conditionally rendered when quantity > 1
patterns.weight_calculation_pattern: Utility functions in lib/utils/weight.ts that operate on grams

existing_implementations.description: Quantity field exists in types and database but NOT in UI. Types show quantity: number (GearItem) and quantity: string (GearItemFormData) with default '1'. Weight calculation utilities exist but don't multiply by quantity yet. LoadoutItem type already has quantity field.
